name: WiFi Drone Remote-ID Detector
label: Drone Mesh Mapper — passive WiFi Drone Remote-ID detector
description: colonelpanichacks/drone-mesh-mapper — passively captures WiFi Remote-ID (ASTM F3411) broadcasts from nearby drones and decodes them; node-mode relays detections over Meshtastic. Receive-only: no transmit, jam, or attack (README-confirmed). Blue-team counter-surveillance. MIT. Cyber Controller flashes the upstream image and adds no capability of its own.

firmware_urls.note: Prebuilt bins are committed in the repo tree (firmware/), no GitHub Release. Fetched pinned to commit efe96b6 from raw.githubusercontent.com and SHA-256-verified at flash time, never vendored.

assets.name: esp32c3-wifi-rid-node-mode.bin
assets.label: Xiao C3 — detector + Meshtastic node relay

assets.name: esp32s3-dual-rid-node-mode.bin
assets.label: Xiao S3 — dual-radio detector + Meshtastic node relay

note: Bins are IN-TREE (firmware/), NOT release assets, and there are 0 tagged releases -> pinned_release at the pinned commit efe96b6 (raw.githubusercontent). Only merged app bins ship (no separate bootloader/partitions in firmware/), so offset 0x0 / merged is the only structurally-valid read; kept SHA verify: (no SHA256SUMS published) -> verify_sha256 REFUSES a mismatch, so real digests MUST be computed from the pinned commit and the 0x0-merged boot confirmed on a real Xiao C3/S3 before any flash (Phase-F HW gate). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES the repo's esp32s3-dual-rid-apple-maps.bin (an Apple-Find-My variant) pending a receive-only confirmation — this profile ships only the 4 pure RX Remote-ID detector images. License is README-declared MIT (GitHub API returns null only because there is no LICENSE *file*); conservatively fetch-from-origin only. danger '' (RX-only) — re-confirm no-TX on hardware.
